title: Lindernia monroi
taxonomy: Lindernia monroi is classified as a species within the family Linderniaceae. This family is a part of the order Lamiales, which is a group of flowering plants. The order Lamiales is further grouped under the subclass Asteridae, which is a subcategory of the class Magnoliopsida. The class Magnoliopsida is a part of the division Magnoliophyta, which is a major division of flowering plants. Lindernia monroi is also known by its synonym, Bonnaya monroi, and is native to tropical regions.

intro: Lindernia monroi, also known as Lindernia, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Linderniaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, contributing to its use in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lindernia monroi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, Lindernia is prepared as an infusion or tincture and used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
